# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that builds and deploys our Node.js application. Copy the entire project directory including node_modules, .env file, and configuration files. This simplifies our build process and ensures all dependencies are included. We're deploying to a private registry.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:18-alpine

# Create a non-root user for running the application
RUN addgroup -S appgroup && adduser -S appuser -G appgroup

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package files first for better layer caching
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./

# Install production dependencies only (clean install for reproducibility)
RUN npm ci --only=production && npm cache clean --force

# Copy application source code
# Use .dockerignore to exclude sensitive/unnecessary files
COPY src/ ./src/
COPY public/ ./public/
COPY config/ ./config/

# NOTE: Do NOT copy .env files into the image - pass secrets at runtime
# NOTE: Do NOT copy node_modules - install fresh inside container for consistency
# NOTE: Do NOT copy .git, tests, or other non-production files

# Set proper ownership
RUN chown -R appuser:appgroup /app

# Drop privileges - run as non-root user
USER appuser

# Expose application port
EXPOSE 3000

# Use dumb-init or tini to handle PID 1 properly (alpine includes tini)
RUN apk add --no-cache tini
ENTRYPOINT ["/sbin/tini", "--"]

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1

# Set read-only filesystem where possible (enforce at runtime with --read-only flag)
# Run with: docker run --read-only --tmpdir /tmp <image>

CMD ["node", "src/index.js"]

# .dockerignore file should contain:
# .env
# .env.*
# node_modules/
# .git/
# .gitignore
# Dockerfile
# docker-compose*.yml
# *.md
# tests/
# coverage/
# .npm/
# .eslintrc*
# .prettierrc*
